title: cerastium gibraltaricum var. vulgare
intro: Cerastium gibraltaricum var. vulgare, also known as the common chickweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, common chickweed has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of common chickweed include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations of common chickweed can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is also used in salves and ointments to promote wound healing and skin rejuvenation.
